summary: The fix adequately addresses both blocking issues from the original voice-stt-server review. (1) stt.test.ts now imports and mounts sttRoutes from the actual ./stt.js module, eliminating the duplicated inline route handlers in both the unit and integration test suites. (2) server.ts now includes express.raw({ type: 'audio/*', limit: '50mb' }) middleware scoped to /api/stt, and stt.ts was updated to use req.body from the middleware instead of manual stream reading. All STT tests pass, typecheck passes, lint passes. One minor test discrepancy noted: test setup includes express.raw() but not express.json(), whereas the real server has express.json() registered first (harmless since express.json() skips audio content types, but worth noting for completeness).

issues.description: Test suite (stt.test.ts) configures express.raw() but not express.json(), whereas the real server.ts has express.json() registered before express.raw(). While this is functionally fine since express.json() will skip audio/wav content types and not consume the stream, the test setup does not perfectly mirror the production middleware stack.
issues.fixSuggestion: Add app.use(express.json()) before app.use(express.raw(...)) in both test beforeEach blocks to exactly match server.ts, ensuring no future middleware ordering regressions are missed.

fixAdequacy.details: Original blocking issue 1 (inline handlers in stt.test.ts): RESOLVED. Both the unit test suite (describe 'STT Routes') and integration test suite (describe 'STT Routes - integration') now use 'app.use(\'/api/stt\', sttRoutes)' instead of duplicating route logic inline. Original blocking issue 2 (no express.raw() middleware in server.ts): RESOLVED. server.ts now has app.use('/api/stt', express.raw({ type: 'audio/*', limit: '50mb' })) registered after express.json(). Additionally, stt.ts was updated to use req.body from the middleware instead of the fragile manual stream reading with for-await-of.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill's TDD procedure was followed correctly by the fix worker (unlike the original voice-stt-server which deviated). The worker ran tests after each change and all STT tests passed (7 tests), typecheck passed, lint passed.
